significance: Anthropic's current top-tier release, aimed at heavy-duty agentic coding and extended reasoning tasks, ships with adaptive thinking on by default (it can't be switched off) and a 1M-token context window out of the box. It became generally available on June 9, 2026 across the Claude API, Claude Platform on AWS, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry, alongside a Project-Glasswing-restricted sibling, Claude Mythos 5. Anthropic's own models-overview guidance now directs developers who need the ceiling of available capability toward Fable 5 rather than Opus 4.8 -- a signal that Fable 5, not Opus 4.8, now sits atop Anthropic's publicly available lineup.

DeepSeek's newest flagship reasoning and agentic model extends default context to 1 million tokens and, per Lab DeepSeek, approaches the performance of leading closed frontier models while remaining open-weight and far cheaper to run via API.

Ai2 describes Olmo 3.1 -- an extended reinforcement-learning follow-up to the Olmo 3 family it first shipped on Nov 20, 2025 -- as its best-performing fully open 32B checkpoints to date for both reasoning (Think) and instruction-following (Instruct) use, citing internal evaluation gains over the original Olmo 3 32B models of over 5 points on AIME and over 20 points on IFBench.

NVIDIA's newest and largest Nemotron 3 release, Ultra is a 550-billion-parameter (55 billion active) Mixture-of-Experts hybrid Mamba-Attention model built for long-running agentic workloads. NVIDIA's own benchmarks put its inference throughput at 1.6x to 5.9x that of three comparably-sized open models, varying by which one it's measured against, at matching accuracy, plus strong long-context retrieval at its 1M-token window.
